title: allium bucharicum
intro: Allium bucharicum, also known as Bukhara onion or Bukhara garlic, is a species of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family, native to Central Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Allium bucharicum include reducing inflammation, lowering cholesterol levels, and acting as an antioxidant. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
